title: Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for OCD and Its Subtypes
book: Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for OCD and Its Subtypes

node_types.description: An individual receiving or providing mental health care (client/patient or therapist).

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role of the person (e.g., client, therapist)

properties.name: gender
properties.description: Gender identity of the person

properties.name: age
properties.description: Age of the person

properties.name: cultural_background
properties.description: Cultural or religious background, including Islamic identity if relevant

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions

node_types.description: A clinical diagnosis or classification relevant to mental health (e.g., OCD, comorbid disorders, subtypes).

properties.name: diagnosis_name
properties.description: Name of the diagnosis (e.g., OCD, depression, tic disorder)

properties.name: onset_age
properties.description: Age at onset of the diagnosis

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Severity rating or description

properties.name: insight_level
properties.description: Level of insight (good, poor, absent/delusional)

properties.name: specifiers
properties.description: Diagnostic specifiers (e.g., tic-related, subtype)

properties.name: cultural_factors
properties.description: Cultural or religious factors influencing diagnosis

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

node_types.description: A specific symptom, obsession, compulsion, or related experience.

properties.name: symptom_type
properties.description: Type of symptom (e.g., obsession, compulsion, avoidance, neutralization)

properties.name: content
properties.description: Content or theme of the symptom (e.g., contamination, harm, religious)

properties.name: frequency
properties.description: Frequency of the symptom

properties.name: intensity
properties.description: Intensity or distress level

properties.name: duration
properties.description: Duration of the symptom

properties.name: insight
properties.description: Insight into the symptom

properties.name: ego_dystonicity
properties.description: Degree to which the symptom is ego-dystonic

properties.name: cultural_context
properties.description: Cultural or religious context of the symptom

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

node_types.description: A cognitive belief, appraisal, or process relevant to OCD and its treatment.

properties.name: construct_type
properties.description: Type of cognitive construct (e.g., inflated responsibility, thought-action fusion, perfectionism, intolerance of uncertainty, overvalued ideation)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the construct

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Severity or prominence in the case

properties.name: cultural_relevance
properties.description: Cultural or religious relevance (e.g., scrupulosity, Islamic beliefs)

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates
CHAPTER 5 The Cognitive-Behavioral Model - THEORY AND RESEARCH
CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

node_types.description: A standardized or idiographic tool used for assessment or measurement.

properties.name: tool_name
properties.description: Name of the assessment tool (e.g., YBOCS, OBQ-44, DPSS)

properties.name: target_construct
properties.description: What the tool measures (e.g., symptom severity, cognitive beliefs)

properties.name: format
properties.description: Format of the tool (e.g., interview, self-report, questionnaire)

properties.name: cultural_adaptation
properties.description: Cultural or religious adaptation, if any

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates

node_types.description: A structured treatment protocol or intervention approach.

properties.name: protocol_name
properties.description: Name of the protocol (e.g., ERP, CBT, DIRT, ACT, mindfulness)

properties.name: components
properties.description: Key components or steps of the protocol

properties.name: target_symptoms
properties.description: Symptoms or subtypes targeted

properties.name: cultural_considerations
properties.description: Cultural or religious considerations in protocol adaptation

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 4 Exposure and Response Prevention - THEORY AND PRACTICE
CHAPTER 5 The Cognitive-Behavioral Model - THEORY AND RESEARCH
CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

node_types.description: A case-specific conceptualization integrating symptoms, cognitive constructs, and treatment planning.

properties.name: primary_obsessions
properties.description: Primary obsessions identified in the case

properties.name: faulty_appraisals
properties.description: Key maladaptive beliefs or appraisals

properties.name: neutralization_strategies
properties.description: Compulsions or strategies used to manage obsessions

properties.name: treatment_plan
properties.description: Planned interventions and rationale

properties.name: cultural_context
properties.description: Cultural or religious context relevant to the case

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

node_types.description: The quality and features of the relationship between therapist and client.

properties.name: alliance_quality
properties.description: Quality of the therapeutic alliance

properties.name: collaboration_level
properties.description: Degree of collaboration and shared decision-making

properties.name: ruptures
properties.description: Noted ruptures or threats to the relationship

properties.name: cultural_alignment
properties.description: Degree of cultural or religious alignment or sensitivity

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has received a specific diagnosis.

properties.name: diagnosis_date
properties.description: Date of diagnosis

properties.name: diagnosed_by
properties.description: Clinician or tool used for diagnosis

edge_types.description: Links a diagnosis to its associated symptoms.

properties.name: symptom_onset
properties.description: Onset of the symptom relative to diagnosis

properties.name: symptom_severity
properties.description: Severity of the symptom in this diagnosis

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a symptom is underpinned or maintained by a specific cognitive construct or belief.

properties.name: evidence_level
properties.description: Strength of evidence for this link in the case

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates
CHAPTER 5 The Cognitive-Behavioral Model - THEORY AND RESEARCH
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person experiences a specific symptom.

properties.name: first_reported
properties.description: Date or context of first report

properties.name: current_status
properties.description: Current status (active, remitted, etc.)

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person exhibits a particular cognitive belief or process.

properties.name: assessment_tool
properties.description: Tool or method used to assess the construct

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2 Obsessions, Intrusions, and Their Correlates
CHAPTER 5 The Cognitive-Behavioral Model - THEORY AND RESEARCH

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person was assessed using a specific tool.

properties.name: assessment_date
properties.description: Date of assessment

properties.name: assessment_result
properties.description: Summary or score from the assessment

edge_types.description: Indicates that a diagnosis was established or supported using a specific assessment tool.

properties.name: assessment_date
properties.description: Date of assessment

properties.name: assessment_result
properties.description: Summary or score from the assessment

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is receiving or has received a specific treatment protocol.

properties.name: start_date
properties.description: Start date of the treatment

properties.name: end_date
properties.description: End date of the treatment

properties.name: treatment_outcome
properties.description: Outcome or response to the treatment

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 4 Exposure and Response Prevention - THEORY AND PRACTICE
CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment protocol targets a specific symptom or symptom subtype.

properties.name: treatment_rationale
properties.description: Rationale for targeting this symptom

properties.name: evidence_base
properties.description: Summary of empirical support

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 4 Exposure and Response Prevention - THEORY AND PRACTICE
CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions
CHAPTER 12 Harm, Sex, and Religious Obsessions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a case formulation informs or guides the selection of a treatment protocol.

properties.name: formulation_date
properties.description: Date of formulation

properties.name: treatment_rationale
properties.description: Rationale for treatment selection

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 8 Goals, Education, and Cognitive Interventions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is involved in a therapeutic relationship (as client or therapist).

properties.name: relationship_start
properties.description: Start date of the therapeutic relationship

properties.name: relationship_status
properties.description: Current status (active, terminated, ruptured, etc.)
